title: Market Brief — Sunday, April 26, 2026
summary: H100 availability sits at a severe 10% (93/324 GPUs), the tightest class in the stack, while H200 at 30% (64/331) and B200 at 27% (168/421) offer moderate relief. B200 posted the biggest daily move at +3.1%, signaling rising demand as utilization surged 29pp in 30 days to 73%, while H100 utilization hit 90.4% confirming the squeeze.
paragraphs: H100 availability sits at a severe 10% (93/324 GPUs), the tightest class in the stack, while H200 at 30% (64/331) and B200 at 27% (168/421) offer moderate relief. B200 posted the biggest daily move at +3.1%, signaling rising demand as utilization surged 29pp in 30 days to 73%, while H100 utilization hit 90.4% confirming the squeeze.

B200 is the only GPU where medium-tier inference roughly breaks even: TCO-adjusted cost is $3.78/M tokens versus $3.02 raw. On H200 at TCO-adjusted $8.46/M, anyone selling at the medium $3/M tier is burning at a loss of roughly $5.46 per million tokens. DeepSeek V3.2 at $0.378/M output is selling far below any self-hosted cost floor. Grades: OpenAI Strong, Anthropic Neutral, Google Neutral, DeepSeek Burning Cash.

H200 is overvalued for inference buyers given 73.5% annualized price volatility, negative margins at most tiers, and declining utilization down 9.3pp. B200 compute at $4.13/hr spot with rapidly climbing utilization and the best margin profile is the clear long; accumulate B200 capacity and sell H100 exposure into the scarcity premium.
bullets: 10% of H100 GPUs are available, a severe shortage with only 93 of 324 free.
27% of B200 GPUs are available with 168 of 421 units free, the deepest liquid pool.
73.5% annualized volatility on H200 OCPI makes it the riskiest compute asset.
$0.378 per million output tokens makes DeepSeek V3.2 the cheapest API endpoint across all 18 models tracked.
21.41 on the LLMflation Index means API pricing has fallen roughly 79% from the GPT-4 launch baseline.
90.4% H100 utilization is the highest across all GPU classes, up 4.7pp in 30 days.
